summary: Extracted positive product mentions from HN frontpage stories.

items.name: DaVinci Resolve
items.summary: Blackmagic expanded DaVinci Resolve with a photo workflow, and HN users framed it as a serious Adobe alternative with a one-time license.
items.why_included: Repeated praise for Resolve's value and color workflow made it the clear concrete tool surfaced by the thread.
items.evidence: Commenters highlighted the $295 perpetual license and said Resolve remains a go-to editor for high-quality video work.

items.name: Jellyfin
items.source_story_title: WiiFin – Jellyfin Client for Nintendo Wii
items.summary: Open-source media server that HN users kept recommending as the escape hatch from Plex lock-in and forced accounts.
items.why_included: The WiiFin thread turned into a broader Jellyfin recommendation and comparison discussion.
items.evidence: Multiple comments praised Jellyfin's self-hosted freedom while recommending a safe remote-access layer instead of exposing it directly.

items.name: Infuse
items.source_story_title: WiiFin – Jellyfin Client for Nintendo Wii
items.summary: Premium Apple-platform player repeatedly cited as the smoothest client for Jellyfin and Plex libraries.
items.why_included: Mentioned as the client that fixes playback pain once the media server is already in place.
items.evidence: HN users called Infuse the best premium Apple client and said it handled files that other apps struggled with.

items.name: Tailscale
items.source_story_title: WiiFin – Jellyfin Client for Nintendo Wii
items.summary: Zero-config mesh VPN HN users recommended for secure remote access to a home media server.
items.why_included: It surfaced as the practical answer for exposing Jellyfin safely without opening it directly to the internet.
items.evidence: Commenters recommended Tailscale as the easiest safe remote-access layer for family media streaming.

items.name: TanStack Start
items.source_story_title: TanStack Start Now Support React Server Components
items.summary: TanStack Start's new React Server Components support triggered strong interest as a leaner alternative to Next.js.
items.why_included: The thread focused on developer ergonomics and lower resource use, making Start the concrete tool people were evaluating.
items.evidence: HN users compared TanStack plus Vite favorably against heavier Next.js setups and highlighted the new RSC support.

items.name: Cloudflare CLI
items.source_story_title: Building a CLI for all of Cloudflare
items.summary: Cloudflare's new unified cf CLI stood out as an agent-friendly local entry point for managing Cloudflare services.
items.why_included: The discussion centered on a concrete new tool rather than a vague platform announcement.
items.evidence: Commenters focused on the new cf preview, permissions ergonomics, and the idea of a consistent CLI for humans and agents.
